message:
perf(simd_scan): add ESC-free fast path and dense-log benchmarks

Optimize the SIMD scanner for the common case where terminal output
contains no ANSI escape sequences, which is the dominant pattern in
operator log streams. The fast path avoids entering the scalar ANSI
parsing loop entirely when no ESC byte (0x1b) is present.

Scanner optimizations (simd_scan.rs):
- scan_newlines_and_ansi_memchr(): if no 0x1b byte exists in the
  chunk, return ansi_byte_count=0 immediately without entering the
  scalar fallback loop — pure memchr newline counting only
- scan_newlines_and_ansi_memchr_with_state(): when no escape carry-
  state and no ESC in the chunk, skip the full ANSI walk and only
  update UTF-8 pending state via new scan_utf8_pending_only() helper
- scan_utf8_pending_only(): short-circuits on ASCII-only input (no
  bytes >= 0x80), otherwise walks only continuation bytes
- Scalar ANSI walk now starts from the first ESC position instead of
  byte 0, skipping already-counted newlines before the first escape
- Add proptest (200 cases) validating stateful fast path matches
  scalar output for random byte streams with random initial state
- Add two targeted unit tests for ESC-free fast path with pending
  UTF-8 carry-state

Benchmarks (benches/simd_scan.rs):
- Add payload_dense_logs() generator simulating realistic operator
  logs: mostly ASCII lines with sparse ANSI escapes every ~64 lines
- Switch chunked stateful benchmark from payload_ansi_heavy to
  payload_dense_logs for more representative real-world measurement
- Update budget description to "should beat scalar by >2x on dense
  ASCII logs"

Property tests (tests/proptest_simd_scan.rs):
- Add ansi_boundary_splits_preserve_metrics (220 cases): ANSI-heavy
  data split at arbitrary boundaries produces identical metrics to
  single-pass scan — validates correctness of the stateful carry API

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
